results.name: Conflicting Requirements & Constraints
results.rawText:
Model Characterisation
- System purpose: The model defines a very simple requirement set for a physical system whose external form must be a 1 m cube, made primarily from 2024 Aluminium, while remaining at or below 1 kg. It also includes interpretation and governing requirements intended to resolve an apparent feasibility conflict.
- Operational domain: Generic physical/mechanical product domain; no specific industry context is stated.
- Lifecycle phase: Early concept / requirements definition. The model is requirement-centric and does not include design, behaviour, interface, or verification detail.
- Model scope: Narrow requirements fragment, focused on shape, material, mass, and conflict-resolution rationale.
- Known scope limitations: No structural model, no parametrics, no allocations, no interfaces, no behaviour/state machines, no safety model, no environmental constraints, and no verification cases are present. Checks depending on those artifacts are assessed as out of scope and passed only insofar as no conflicting evidence exists in this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 1. Numeric Constraint Conflicts - The only explicit numeric bound is "no more than 1 kg"; no second explicit numeric constraint on the same quantified attribute is given, and no parametric equations or subcomponent budgets are present that create a demonstrable numeric contradiction in the model text.

CHECK PASSED: 2. Behavioural Conflicts - No state machines, activities, transitions, triggers, liveness clauses, or timing-execution allocations are present in this requirements fragment, so no behavioural conflict can be evidenced here.

CHECK PASSED: 3. Interface & Protocol Conflicts - The model contains no ports, connections, item flows, interface definitions, or protocol specifications, so no interface/protocol conflict is present within the assessed scope.

CHECK PASSED: 4. Requirement-Level Conflicts - The apparent tension among Shape, Material, and Weight is explicitly addressed by ShapeInterpretation, LightweightConstruction, StructuralMassAllocation, and governing priority/rationale annotations; within the model text, this is presented as a resolved design intent rather than an unresolved contradiction.

CHECK PASSED: 5. Allocation Conflicts - No functional, logical, software, or physical allocations are modeled, so there is no evidence of conflicting allocations in this fragment.

CHECK PASSED: 6. Safety vs. Functional Conflicts - No safety constraints, hazard responses, watchdogs, or functional behaviors are modeled, so no safety-functional conflict can be established from the provided content.

CHECK PASSED: 7. Priority & Resolution - The model documents priority ordering and rationale through PriorityAnnotatedRequirement and the Governing* requirements, providing an explicit resolution mechanism for the otherwise apparent requirement tension.

| ID | Score | Confidence | One-line description |
|---|---:|---|---|
| None | 1.0 | HIGH | No unresolved conflicting requirements or constraints were found in the provided model fragment. |

Overall Assessment Score: 1.00
